request.system:
You extract keyed dungeon areas from tabletop adventure module pages. The user message names the areas to extract, then interleaves each page's extracted text (each headed by a [page N] marker) with that page's image. Return one entry per named area, using exactly the canonical keys given.

Rules:
- "encounters" are the area's hostile or monstrous inhabitants with stat blocks. Ordinary townsfolk and named NPCs without a creature type are not encounters — they belong in the description. Use the creature type as printed ("goblin"), putting proper names ("Snagg") in the description. Wandering-monster tables are not keyed encounters.
- Encounter counts: put a plain number in "count_fixed" and dice notation like "2d4" in "count_dice"; anything the module says about count that fits neither (rates, conditions, ranges) goes in "count_note". Leave unused count fields null.
- "trap" describes the area's trap, null when it has none. "features" lists notable fixtures a referee should know about (altars, pools, levers, secret doors); "treasure" lists the area's treasure as printed.
- "connections": derive each connection's "direction" from the level map when the text is silent; use "unknown" only when neither the text nor the map says. For "to_key", use the connected area's canonical key when it appears in the area list; otherwise its printed label.
- "source_pages" refer to the [page N] markers in this request, never to page numbers printed on the pages.
- "confidence" is your self-assessment in [0, 1] of how faithfully you extracted that area.

parts.text:
Extract the keyed areas listed below from dungeon "J. Gnoll Lair" (id: j-gnoll-lair), level 1.
Areas to extract (canonical key | printed key | name | expected pages):
- 69 | printed key "69" | Entrance | pages 36
- 70 | printed key "70" | Guard Post | pages 36
- 71 | printed key "71" | Guard Post | pages 36
- 72 | printed key "72" | Common Room | pages 36
- 73 | printed key "73" | Cavern | pages 36
- 74 | printed key "74" | Leader's Room | pages 36
- treasure-hoard | printed key "TREASURE HOARD" | Treasure Hoard | pages 37
- 75 | printed key "75" | Storeroom | pages 37
- 76 | printed key "76" | Dungeon | pages 37
This level's map is on page 38.

[page 36]
The Caves The Chaotic Caves
J. GNOLL LAIR
These gnolls have been brought here by the
scent of loot and fresh meat.
They are armed with wide-bladed, heavy
swords of their own making; others who wish
to wield them must have at least 12 Strength
to do so.
69. ENTRANCE: This entranceway seems to be
unguarded. A dead goblin and two dead
bugbears lie sprawled just outside it. They
have been dead for several days and have not
been disturbed, beyond some nibbling by small
animals and birds. The place is quiet and
reeks of desolation; any tracks that are found
are days old.
Which is a trap: gnolls from the guard posts at
#70 and #71 keep watch around the corner at
location "w". Based on what any intruders do,
the gnolls attempt an end-round. If intruders
explore the door to #70, gnolls from that room
leave and come charging down the hall from
the west opening ("w"). If they bypass the
door, gnolls exit the door and attack. Those at
#71 will remain in reserve and make crafty use
of the well-concealed secret door to come out
behind opponents and attack if they can.
70. GUARD POST: Three gnolls wait here – one
is on duty at the corner at the area marked "w"
(along with the one from room #71) and will
alert the rest of those in this room if it detects
intruders. Most of their furniture is shabby,
although three sections of a large tree have
been carved into crude benches. Each gnoll
has 1d6 ep.
3 Gnolls: AC 15, HD 2, #At 1 heavy sword, 
Dam 2d4, Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
71. GUARD POST: Three gnolls wait here – one
is on duty at the corner at the area marked "w"
(along with the one from room #70) and will
alert the ones in this room if intruders are
spotted. They have shabby furniture. Each
gnoll has 1d6 ep.
3 Gnolls: AC 15, HD 2, #At 1 heavy sword, 
Dam 2d4, Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
72. COMMON ROOM: A group of gnolls lounge
in this room, often bored and bickering. A pet
wolf is tied by rope to the northwest corner,
fed scraps, and trained as both guard and war
dog. It has a high chance of hearing or
smelling anyone who spends too much time
outside the doors debating what to do. Each
gnoll has 1d6 ep. The unmarked room to the
southwest contains crude cots.
5 Gnolls: AC 15, HD 2, #At 1 heavy sword, 
Dam 2d4, Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
Wolf: AC 13, HD 2, #At 1 bite, Dam 1d6, Mv 60', 
Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
73. CAVERN: This natural cavern is divided by
stone columns. There is nothing here, although
the dripping of water provides some pools of
clean drinking water.
74. LEADER'S ROOM: The gnoll leader resides
here along with two mates. The leader wields
a Battle Axe +1 and fights fiercely. It wears a
golden necklace with gems worth 700 gp.
The room has a lot of junk in it, including a
collection of clay jars (drinking size), many of
them smashed. One jar contains two keys tied
together: they open the treasure room to the
south and the store room at #75.
Gnoll leader: AC 15, HD 6, #At 1 battle axe +1, 
Dam 1d8+2, Mv 30', Sv F6, ML 8, XP 500
HP 27 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐
2 Gnoll Females: AC 15, HD 2, #At 1 heavy 
sword, Dam 2d4, Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
34

[page 37]
The Chaotic Caves The Caves
TREASURE HOARD: The door is trapped by a
heavy flint axe-head on a strong wooden bar
that swings down (it attacks as 3 HD monster,
doing 1d8+2 points of damage if it hits). 
The leader keeps a giant black widow spider
here, feeding it rats and kobolds as needed,
and cleaning up after it. It lurks overhead in
webs which are so covered by dust that they
appear to be the ceiling, about 10' up (the
actual ceiling is 15' high). It had a bad
experience with the ax trap and avoids it.
A strong chest holds 639 gp, 533 sp, and 617
cp. Other boxes hold assorted goods including
food, raw cloth in bolts (about 6, each worth
perhaps 1 to 5 gp), and other items.
Giant Black Widow Spider: AC 14, HD 3*, 
#At 1 bite, Dam 2d6 + poison, Mv 20' 40' web, 
Sv F3, ML 8, XP 175
HP 12 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐
75. STOREROOM. The door is locked and the
gnoll leader in room #74 has the key. Barrels,
boxes, and bags are crammed into this store
room. Most contain food or cheap ale.
76. DUNGEON: A gnoll is on guard with the key
(it has 1d6 ep). Captives being kept for ransom
are held here. The cell "doors" are made of
thick wooden bars with an inch gap between
each and have iron hinges and locks. The locks
are not high quality and a Thief would have a
high chance of picking them. There are no
prisoners here now.
Cell "a" is a common cell. Cell "b" is for
anyone they wish to segregate. Area "c" is
reached by climbing rough-cut steps some 10'
up. It contains a torture area, chopping block,
hand axe, wooden rack, several wooden "saw
horses", and other items. A pile of coal lies
beside an iron brazier and with it are crude
iron pokers of various types.
Gnoll: AC 15, HD 2, #At 1 heavy sword, 
Dam 2d4, Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 8, XP 75
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
35

areas.description: The entrance appears unguarded. A dead goblin and two dead bugbears lie outside, dead for several days and only nibbled by small animals and birds. The place is quiet, reeks of desolation, and any tracks are days old. This is a setup for an ambush: gnolls from areas 70 and 71 watch from around the corner at location "w." Depending on intruder actions, those from 70 may charge from the west opening or come out through their door, while those from 71 may use a well-concealed secret door to attack from behind.
areas.trap: Hidden gnoll ambush coordinated from areas 70 and 71; watchers at location "w" observe intruders, with gnolls from 70 attacking from the west opening or their room door and gnolls from 71 using a concealed secret door to strike from behind.
features: Well-concealed secret door used by gnolls from 71

areas.description: A shabby guard post with three crude benches carved from sections of a large tree. One gnoll is posted at location "w" with a gnoll from area 71 and alerts the others here if intruders are detected. Each gnoll carries 1d6 ep.
features: Three crude benches carved from large tree sections

areas.description: A shabby guard post occupied by three gnolls. One stands watch at location "w" alongside a gnoll from area 70 and alerts the others here if intruders are spotted. Each gnoll carries 1d6 ep.
features: Secret door used for flanking attacks

areas.description: A common room where a group of gnolls lounge, usually bored and bickering. A pet wolf is tied by rope to the northwest corner, fed scraps, and trained as both a guard and war dog; it is likely to hear or smell anyone lingering outside the doors. Each gnoll has 1d6 ep. The unmarked room to the southwest contains crude cots.

encounters.count_note: Pet wolf tied by rope in the northwest corner

features: Pet wolf tied by rope to the northwest corner
Unmarked room to the southwest with crude cots

areas.description: A natural cavern divided by stone columns. It is otherwise empty, though dripping water has formed pools of clean drinking water.

The gnoll leader lives here with two mates. The leader fights with a Battle Axe +1 and wears a golden necklace set with gems worth 700 gp. The room is cluttered with junk, including many clay drinking jars, many smashed. One jar contains two keys tied together; they open the treasure room to the south and the storeroom at area 75.
treasure: Golden necklace with gems worth 700 gp
Two keys tied together opening the treasure room and area 75
features: Collection of clay drinking jars, many smashed

areas.description: The gnoll leader keeps a giant black widow spider here, feeding it rats and kobolds and cleaning up after it. The spider lurks overhead in dusty webs about 10 feet up; the real ceiling is 15 feet high. It had a bad experience with the door trap and avoids it. A strong chest and other boxes hold coins and assorted goods.
areas.trap: The door is trapped with a heavy flint axe-head mounted on a strong wooden bar that swings down; it attacks as a 3 HD monster and deals 1d8+2 damage on a hit.
treasure: Strong chest with 639 gp, 533 sp, and 617 cp
About 6 bolts of raw cloth, each worth perhaps 1 to 5 gp

areas.description: A locked storeroom crammed with barrels, boxes, and bags. The gnoll leader in area 74 has the key. Most of the contents are food or cheap ale.
features: Barrels, boxes, and bags crammed into the room
Stores of food and cheap ale

areas.description: A gnoll stands guard here with the key and has 1d6 ep. The area is used to hold captives for ransom, though there are no prisoners at present. The cell doors are thick wooden bars with one-inch gaps, fitted with iron hinges and locks; the locks are poor quality and easy for a thief to pick. Cell "a" is a common cell, cell "b" is for segregated prisoners, and area "c" is reached by climbing rough-cut steps about 10 feet up. Area "c" contains a torture area with a chopping block, hand axe, wooden rack, several wooden sawhorses, a pile of coal beside an iron brazier, and crude iron pokers of various types.
features: Thick wooden-bar cell doors with 1-inch gaps
Iron hinges and locks of poor quality
Rough-cut steps leading 10' up to area "c"
